news.t: These alternative index strategies are beating the S&P 500 after the stock market's new highs
Love your stock-index fund? It might be time to spice up your investment strategy.
The S&P 500 is too tech-heavy. This alternative ETF keeps beating it.
This fund beats the S&P 500 by using just 75 of its components. Here's how it works.

holdings.infoBox: SPGP is an equity ETF with a total of 76 individual holdings. The top holdings are Host Hotels & Resorts stock at 3.08%, Monolithic Power Systems at 2.98%, Fortinet at 2.85%, NVIDIA at 2.72%, and Delta Air Lines at 2.67%.

overview.description: The Invesco S&P 500 GARP ETF (SPGP) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the S&P 500 GARP index. The fund tracks an index of S&P 500 stocks selected by growth, quality and value traits, and weighted by growth. SPGP was launched on Jun 16, 2011 and is issued by Invesco.

data.title: These alternative index strategies are beating the S&P 500 after the stock market's new highs
data.text: Invesco has nine factor approaches to tracking the S&P 500. All but one have beaten the index this year, and two have stood out since the stock market's March 30 low.

data.title: Love your stock-index fund? It might be time to spice up your investment strategy.
data.text: There are easy and inexpensive ways to broaden your stock market exposure, even within the S&P 500.

data.title: The S&P 500 is too tech-heavy. This alternative ETF keeps beating it.
data.text: The various stock indexing and weighting schemes will shine at different times, but some approaches are designed to be immune from market cycles.

data.title: This fund beats the S&P 500 by using just 75 of its components. Here's how it works.
data.text: What worked well during the years-long bull market through 2021 — a focus on growth, regardless of price — has ground to a halt this year. The rebirth of the value style of investing — and modest valu...
